summary: Salary Calculator Auto-Calculate Bug Fix VERIFIED. The fix for employee.get('salary_info', {}) returning None has been confirmed working. Both backend API and frontend UI tests passed successfully.

features_tested.details: Successfully logged in with superadmin@bijnisbooks.com / SuperAdmin@123
Found in HR MANAGEMENT section, page loads correctly with all components
After clicking Auto-Calculate: Total Monthly Salary = 25000, Daily Allowance Rate = 200, Working Days = 26, Absent Days = 1
Shows: 'Auto-Calculated from 7 Attendance Records', Present Days: 6, Absent Days: 1, Leave Days: 0, Late Hours: 1.5 hrs, Recent Late Entries with dates
Final salary displayed as ₹18,997.21 with calculation breakdown
All 7 pytest tests passed including bug fix verification test

bug_fix_verification.bug_description: employee.get('salary_info', {}) could return None when salary_info is explicitly null in database, causing AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'get'
bug_fix_verification.test_result: PASS - API now handles null salary_info correctly by defaulting to empty dict

ui_test_summary.navigation: WORKING - Salary Calculator found in HR MANAGEMENT section
ui_test_summary.form_auto_population: WORKING - All fields populated after auto-calculate

context_for_next_testing_agent: The Salary Calculator Auto-Calculate feature is fully working. The bug fix for handling null salary_info has been verified. Test Employee (ID: 15083432-22e4-4159-9561-2542231a5e96) has attendance records and salary_info configured for testing. The frontend correctly displays auto-calculated attendance data, salary breakdown, and combined final salary after hourly deductions.
